item.title: Junior scholarship results to be published today
item.description: Results of the Junior Scholarship Examinations will be published today (Wednesday), said officials of the education ministry.......

item.title: Japanese scholars attend DU seminar
item.description: The 2nd international seminar on Regional Input-Output Analysis, jointly organised by the Centre for Trade and Investment of Dhaka University and Ritsumeikan University, Japan, was held at the Faculty of Business Studies on Monday......

item.title: Govt must ensure people’s democratic rights: discussion
item.description: Politicians, professionals and journalists at a discussion in Dhaka on Tuesday said that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party government must work for the country’s democratic transition by ensuring democratic rights......

item.title: PCJSS member killed in ‘gunfight’ with army
item.description: A member of hill people’s organisation Parbatya Chattagram Jana Samhati Samiti was killed in a reported gunfight with the Bangladesh Army at Rowangchhari upazila in Bandarban on Monday afternoon......

item.title: Thailand, Cambodia dispute exchange of fire at border
item.description: Thailand’s military said its troops exchanged fire with Cambodian forces along their border on Tuesday, accusing its neighbour of violating a December truce, while Phnom Penh denied the use of ‘any weapons’......

item.title: Ukraine unbroken after 4 years: Zelensky
item.description: Vladimir Putin has not broken Ukraine, its leader Volodymyr Zelensky said Tuesday, as the Kremlin marked the fourth year of its invasion by vowing that it will keep fighting Europe’s bloodiest conflict since World War II until it achieves its goals......

item.title: Hamas calls for sanctions against Israel over new West Bank moves
item.description: Hamas on Tuesday called for sanctions against Israel, welcoming a joint condemnation by nearly 20 countries of new Israeli measures aimed at tightening control over the occupied West Bank......

item.title: Tarique asks city administrators to set priorities for campaign
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday directed the newly appointed administrators of six city corporations, including the two in Dhaka, to take swift measures to curb the recent spike in mosquito infestations......

item.title: Tarique asks city administrators to set priorities for campaign
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday directed the newly appointed administrators of six city corporations, including the two in Dhaka, to take swift measures to curb the recent spike in mosquito infestations......

item.title: Protests erupt as police beat journalists, DU students
item.description: The police on Monday evening beat up journalists and a Dhaka University student during an anti-narcotics drive in the capital’s Suhrawardy Udyan, drawing widespread criticism and protests demanding the removal of Dhaka Metropolitan Police deputy commissioner for Ramna division Masud Alam......

item.title: Two former NU VCs, ex-DIG face travel ban
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Senior Special Judge Court on Tuesday imposed international travel ban on National University former vice chancellors professor Harun-or-Rashid and Professor Mashiur Rahman over corruption allegations......

item.title: 1,202 more cases cleared for withdrawal
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Tuesday approved the withdrawal of 1,202 more politically motivated cases filed during the 17-year rule of the ousted Awami League regime......

item.title: City mosquito density reaches alarming level
item.description: Mosquito density in Dhaka has increased alarmingly this season, with residents across the capital complaining of severe infestation of the insect and alleging inadequate control measures by the authorities......

item.title: Activities against sovereignty behind BDR massacre: PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday said that the activities against the country’s independence and sovereignty were perceivably involved behind the 2009 Pilkhana massacre......

item.title: Victim families want trial of conspirators
item.description: The families of the victims of the 2009 Bangladesh Rifles mutiny at the erstwhile BDR headquarters in the capital’s Pilkhana area have demanded punishment for the perpetrators and conspirators......

item.title: Akbar, Afif, Mominul: same but different
item.description: Akbar Ali, Afif Hossain, and Mominul Haque—three batters on different spectra of the national team and on different stages of their careers—shone bright on the opening day of the Bangladesh Cricket League one-day version on Monday......

item.title: Govt to allocate three football venues to BFF: Aminul
item.description: State minister for youth and sports Aminul Haque announced on Tuesday that the government has decided to allocate three football venues to be formally handed over to the Bangladesh Football Federation through an agreement......

item.title: Sri Lanka can rescue WC campaign
item.description: Co-hosts Sri Lanka can win both their remaining Super Eight matches to rescue their faltering T20 World Cup campaign, starting with New Zealand, said spinner Dunith Wellalage on Tuesday......

item.title: How fear was engineered by kneecapping dissent
item.description: ‘THE police took me, blindfolded me, and deliberately shot me in the legs. They never informed my family. When my family began searching for me, the police told them I was dead. I am my parents’ only son — imagine their mental state when they heard that.’......

item.title: Will post-July Bangladesh learn from post-July Egypt?
item.description: IN JULY-AUGUST 2024, people around the world were amazed by the courage and heroism of the youth of Bangladesh who launched a movement against the oppressive regime of Sheikh Hasina. Known as the July revolution, it put to an end over 15 years of Hasina’s rule that was marked by massive corruption and human rights violations. Over 1,400 young people......

item.title: Redesigning Dhaka’s transport system
item.description: DHAKA’S transportation system is central to the city’s daily struggle to remain liveable. It is marked by chronic congestion, prolonged commutes, rising travel costs, deteriorating air quality, and mounting strain on residents’ physical and mental well-being. Transport inefficiency is not merely an infrastructure deficit; it reflects weak planning, fragmented governance......

item.title: Aligning growth with clean production
item.description: ON A winter morning in Dhaka, the air can feel like a fogged pane of glass — hazy, weighty, almost routine in its discomfort. We have grown used to reading the sky as an index of our economic life. The same city that struggles to breathe is also the command centre of a production-driven economy built on manufacturing, processing and export. In that tension lies the......

item.title: Repeated gas explosions signal structural neglect
item.description: THE death and critical burn injuries of multiple family members in two gas explosions in Chattogram and Dhaka on February 23 present an alarming reminder of the persistent and lethal risks embedded within the fragile urban gas infrastructure. The incidents, occurring within hours of each other in residential buildings, underscore how routine domestic spaces have......

item.title: Partisan placement risks integrity of local government
item.description: THE appointment of Bangladesh Nationalist Party leaders as administrators to six city corporations — Dhaka South, Dhaka North, Khulna, Sylhet, Narayanganj and Gazipur — warrants careful reflection. After the 2024 mass uprising, elected representatives were removed from 12 city corporations, 330 municipalities, 497 upazila councils and several district councils. Now......

item.title: JS to decide party symbols in local polls: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Tuesday said that the decision on whether local government elections would be held under party symbols would be taken in the parliament......

item.title: Section 144 imposed to avoid BNP’s factional clash
item.description: The Bagharpara Upazila administration imposed restrictions on public gathering within Bagharpara Municipality to avoid untoward incidents following growing tension among two factions of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party......

item.title: Govt reaffirms commitment to revitalising SAARC
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Tuesday reaffirmed Bangladesh’s unwavering commitment to revitalising the SAARC process, expressing readiness to take proactive initiatives to strengthen regional cooperation in South Asia......

item.title: 2 motorcyclists killed, 3 hurt in Sylhet, Habiganj
item.description: Two motorcyclists were killed and 3 others were injured in separate road accidents in Sylhet and Habiganj on Monday night......

item.title: BADC chairman visits projects
item.description: The Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation (BADC) chairman, additional secretary Md Azizul Islam, inspected the activities of the Vapour Heat Treatment Project being implemented by BADC at Gabtoli, the under construction pre-fabricated fertiliser warehouse, and the fertiliser loading-unloading activities of BADC at Amin Bazar in Dhaka on Saturday......Geographic Reference

item.title: UCBD partners with Scholastica
item.description: Universal College Bangladesh has recently signed a memorandum of understanding with Scholastica to support students in planning their future academic paths......

item.title: Revolutionary Guards conduct military drills in Iran’s south
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards, the ideological arm of the military, are carrying out drills on the country’s southern shores of the Gulf, state media said Tuesday......

item.title: Europe faces large shortfalls in its defence: report
item.description: Europe has wide ‘shortfalls’ in its military capabilities which leave it ‘ill-prepared’ as the war in Ukraine grinds on, a new report said Tuesday......

item.title: Selected airports, railway stations to come under free WiFi before Eid
item.description: A meeting at the posts and telecommunications ministry on Tuesday selected a number of airports and railway stations to set up free Wi-Fi facilities before Eid......

item.title: Govt officers, employees asked to strictly follow office hours
item.description: The Cabinet Division on Tuesday instructed officers and employees of all government, semi-government, autonomous and semi-autonomous institutions to strictly follow office hours for arrival and departure......

item.title: ‘The Saline Quest for Honey’ launched
item.description: Based on extensive field research on Sundarbans, a new book titled ‘The Saline Quest for Honey: Living with Tides, Tigers and Traditions’ was launched at a programme at Goethe-Institut Bangladesh of Dhanmondi in the capital on Tuesday......

item.title: Ministries hold meeting for canal digging
item.description: An inter-ministerial meeting was held in the meeting room of the Ministry of Water Resources on Tuesday to implement the nationwide ‘River-Canal-Reservoir Excavation and Re-Excavation’ programme......

item.title: BNP MP’s convoy kills child
item.description: The Islami Andolan Bangladesh demanded the resignation of Bangladesh Nationalist Party lawmaker for the Cox’s Bazar-2 constituency, Almgir Mohammad Mahfuz Ullah Farid, as a child was killed after being hit by a vehicle in his convoy in Chakaria in the district on Sunday......

item.title: Pro-BNP lawyers boycott court as AL leaders granted bail
item.description: A group of pro-BNP lawyers on Tuesday boycotted Barishal courts following the granting of bail to leaders of Awami League, activities of which are banned until the end of the trials of July atrocities......

item.title: Tarique opens Ekushey Book Fair tomorrow
item.description: The Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026 will begin in Suhrawardy Udyan and on Bangla Academy premises tomorrow......
